scene_notes: Central column rebuilt from a real imported NIH3D lumbar vertebra asset, duplicated into a full vertical spine rather than modeled from primitive shapes.
Dense cyan, magenta, blue, and green particle cloud surrounds the central column.
Dark cinematic web-hero composition with reflective highlights and subtle UI text hints.
Latest revision restores the older successful technique: one anatomical vertebra object imported, transformed, multiplied, and styled as wet iridescent black metal.
Color pass tuned toward the reference: darker oil-slick metal base, cyan/blue highlights, magenta surface accents, and green lower particle glow.
Added a cropped texture from the supplied screenshot: assets/reference_card_sustainable_horizons.png.

Duplicated that card to 15 total instances arranged as a true equal-angle, equal-height spiral around the elongated spine. Cards are yawed around the spine so foreground, side, and rear panels form a real orbit rather than a flat billboard layout.
Replaced the old flat backdrop plane with a large inward-facing dark environment sphere named deep_black_world_shell, so the scene supports 360-degree camera orbiting.
Added camera_360_orbit_rig_rotate_z; rotate this empty around Z in Blender to orbit the camera around the spine.
Filled the 360-degree environment with colored star dust, diagonal Milky Way-like ribbons, and small blue-line constellations distributed around the spine instead of painted onto a flat background.
